Operation 'createpayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. Enhanced version specifies: (1) what inputs are expected (card, buyer, basket, addresses from CreatePaymentRequest schema), (2) what is returned (PaymentResponse with transaction status and ID), (3) side effects (immediate payment processing and state change), (4) authentication requirement (IYZWSv2), and (5) clarifies the non-3DS context. This enables AI agents to understand when to use this operation, what data to provide, and what to expect in response.

data.current_description: Creates a payment transaction without 3DS verification.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a non-3DS authenticated payment transaction
data.description_suggestion: Creates a payment transaction without 3DS (Three-Domain Secure) verification. Accepts payment details including card information, buyer information, basket items, and billing/shipping addresses. Returns a PaymentResponse containing transaction status, payment ID, and authorization details. The operation processes the payment immediately and updates the transaction state. Requires IYZWSv2 authentication.

This enables AI agents to understand when to use this operation, what data to provide, and what to expect in response.
